tracks.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
tracks.description: Striver A2Z (TUF) — 18 steps from syntax to advanced strings. Mastery via patterns + spaced re-solving.

materials.title: Striver A2Z sheet — official syllabus
materials.notes: Bookmark. Single source of truth for the 450.

materials.title: User I/O in your language
materials.notes: Pick ONE language. Speed-read syntax.

materials.title: Data types & operators
materials.notes: int/long ranges, overflow gotchas.

materials.title: Time & space complexity primer
materials.notes: Big-O/Theta/Omega. Recognize O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), O(n²), O(2^n).

materials.title: Star patterns (15+ classics)
materials.notes: Pyramids, half-pyramids, hollow, diamond. Build loop intuition.

materials.title: C++ STL or Java Collections
materials.notes: vector/list, pair, map/set, queue/stack, priority_queue, sort/lower_bound. Memorize.

materials.title: Reverse a number
materials.notes: Watch INT_MAX/MIN overflow.

materials.title: GCD/HCF
materials.notes: gcd(a,b) = gcd(b, a%b). Base: b==0.

materials.title: Print all divisors
materials.notes: Loop i to √n. Add i and n/i.

materials.title: Check prime
materials.notes: Loop to √n.

materials.title: Recursion intro — print 1..N
materials.notes: Base case, recursive call. Stack memory.

materials.title: Print name N times via recursion
Fibonacci (recursion + memo)
materials.notes: Naive O(2^n). Memo O(n).

materials.title: Frequency of array elements (hashing)
materials.notes: unordered_map / dict. Foundation for many problems.

materials.title: Selection sort
materials.notes: Pick min from unsorted half. O(n²).

materials.title: Bubble sort
materials.notes: Swap adjacent. O(n²). Early exit if sorted pass.

materials.title: Insertion sort
materials.notes: Insert nums[i] into sorted prefix. Best O(n) for nearly sorted.

materials.title: Merge sort
materials.notes: Divide+conquer. Stable. O(n log n) always. O(n) aux.

materials.title: Quick sort
materials.notes: Partition. Avg O(n log n). Worst O(n²) on sorted unless random pivot.

materials.title: Sort an array (implement)
materials.notes: Implement merge sort end-to-end.

materials.title: Second largest (no sort)
materials.notes: Single pass: track largest + second.

materials.title: Remove duplicates from sorted array
materials.notes: Two pointer (slow/fast). In-place.

materials.title: Left rotate by D places
materials.notes: Reverse trick. k %= n first.

materials.title: Union of two sorted arrays
materials.notes: Two pointer merge, skip dupes.

materials.title: Missing number 0..n
materials.notes: XOR or sum trick.

materials.title: Single number (others twice)
materials.notes: XOR all.

materials.title: Longest subarray sum K (positives)
materials.notes: Sliding window if all positive.

materials.title: Longest subarray sum K (any sign)
materials.notes: Prefix sum + hashmap.

materials.title: Two sum
materials.notes: Hash map. Single pass.

materials.title: Sort colors (0/1/2)
materials.notes: Dutch national flag: low/mid/high.

materials.title: Majority element (>n/2)
materials.notes: Boyer-Moore voting.

materials.title: Kadane — max subarray sum
materials.notes: Foundational. Reset on negative running sum.

materials.title: Print Kadane's subarray
materials.notes: Track start/end indices.

materials.title: Best time to buy & sell stock I
materials.notes: Track min so far, max profit.

materials.title: Next permutation
materials.notes: Classic 4-step. Memorize.

materials.title: Leaders in array
materials.notes: Right-to-left scan, track max so far.

materials.title: Longest consecutive sequence
materials.notes: Hash set. Only start counting from sequence heads.

materials.title: Set matrix zeros
materials.notes: In-place: use first row/col as markers.

materials.title: Rotate matrix 90°
materials.notes: Transpose then reverse rows.

materials.title: Spiral matrix
materials.notes: Four bounds, shrink each pass.

materials.title: Count subarrays sum K
materials.notes: Prefix sum + hash map. Foundational pattern.

materials.title: Pascal's triangle
materials.notes: C(n,r) recursion or direct formula.

materials.title: Majority element (>n/3)
materials.notes: Extended Boyer-Moore: at most 2 candidates.

materials.title: 3 sum
materials.notes: Sort + two pointer. Skip dupes carefully.

materials.title: 4 sum
materials.notes: Sort + double loop + two pointer. Overflow careful.

materials.title: Largest subarray with zero sum
materials.notes: Prefix sum hash map.

materials.title: Count subarrays with XOR K
materials.notes: Prefix XOR map. count(prefix^K).

materials.title: Merge two sorted arrays in-place
materials.notes: Fill from end. Three pointers.

materials.title: Repeating + missing number
materials.notes: Math eqs (sum, sum²) or XOR partition.

materials.title: Find duplicate (n+1 array)
materials.notes: Floyd cycle (array-as-LL). O(n)/O(1).

materials.title: Max product subarray
materials.notes: Track min+max ending at i.

materials.title: Count inversions
materials.notes: Modified merge sort. Count when L[i]>R[j] in merge.

materials.title: Reverse pairs
materials.notes: Like count inversions but condition L[i]>2*R[j]. Two-pointer in merge.

materials.title: Binary search template
materials.notes: Master both inclusive and exclusive variants.

materials.title: Count occurrences in sorted array
materials.notes: UB - LB.

materials.title: Search in rotated sorted array I
materials.notes: One half always sorted.

materials.title: Search in rotated sorted array II (dupes)
Find min in rotated sorted array
Times array has been rotated
materials.notes: Index of min element.

materials.title: Single element in sorted array
materials.notes: Pairs at even index. Single breaks pattern.

materials.title: Koko eating bananas
materials.notes: BS on speed.

materials.title: Minimum days to make M bouquets
Capacity to ship in D days
materials.notes: BS on capacity.

materials.title: Aggressive cows
materials.notes: BS on min distance.

materials.title: Split array — largest sum
materials.notes: BS on max sum allowed.

materials.title: Minimize max distance to gas station
Median of two sorted arrays
materials.notes: Hard. BS on smaller array's partition.

materials.title: Kth element of two sorted arrays
Search in 2D matrix I
materials.notes: Treat as flat sorted. BS on m*n.

materials.title: Search in 2D matrix II (row/col sorted)
materials.notes: Start top-right or bot-left. O(m+n).

materials.title: Peak element in 2D matrix
materials.notes: BS on columns.

materials.title: String rotation check
materials.notes: (s1+s1).contains(s2).

materials.title: Implement atoi
materials.notes: Many edge cases. Whitespace, sign, overflow.

materials.title: Longest palindromic substring
materials.notes: Expand around center.

materials.title: Pairs with sum K in sorted DLL
Reverse LL (iterative)
materials.notes: prev/cur/next dance. Memorize.

materials.title: Find start of loop in LL
Palindrome LL
materials.notes: Reverse second half.

materials.title: Remove Nth from end
materials.notes: Dummy + two pointer gap n+1.

materials.title: Intersection of two LLs
materials.notes: Pointer swap trick.

materials.title: Reverse LL in groups of K
materials.notes: Brutal pointer surgery.

materials.title: Copy LL with random pointer
materials.notes: Interleave or hashmap.

materials.title: Pow(x, n)
materials.notes: Fast exp. Halve recursively. O(log n).

materials.title: Print all subsequences / power set
materials.notes: Pick/not-pick. O(2^n).

materials.title: Count all subsequences with sum K
Check if subseq with sum K exists
N-Queens
materials.notes: Track cols/diag1/diag2.

materials.title: Word break
materials.notes: DP also works.

materials.title: Sudoku solver
materials.notes: Backtracking on each empty cell.

materials.title: Bit manipulation intro (theory)
materials.notes: set/clear/toggle/check. n&(n-1) trick.

materials.title: Count set bits
materials.notes: Brian Kernighan.

materials.title: Divide without using /
materials.notes: Bit-shift subtraction.

materials.title: Count bits to flip A→B
materials.notes: popcount(a^b).

materials.title: Power set via bits
materials.notes: i from 0 to 2^n-1, bit j of i = include nums[j].

materials.title: XOR of L..R
materials.notes: XOR(0..n) follows period-4 pattern.

materials.title: Single number II (one appears once, rest 3×)
Sieve of Eratosthenes
materials.notes: O(n log log n). Memorize.

materials.title: Queue using stacks
materials.notes: Two stacks. Amortized O(1).

materials.title: Next greater element I
materials.notes: The pattern. Memorize.

materials.title: Trapping rainwater
materials.notes: Two pointer OR monotonic stack. Both worth knowing.

materials.title: Remove K digits
materials.notes: Monotonic increasing stack.

materials.title: Maximal rectangle (in matrix)
materials.notes: Histogram per row.

materials.title: Sliding window maximum
materials.notes: Monotonic deque of indices.

materials.title: LRU cache
materials.notes: HashMap + DLL. All O(1).

materials.title: Max consecutive ones III
materials.notes: ≤K zeros window.

materials.title: Binary subarrays with sum
materials.notes: atMost(K)-atMost(K-1).

materials.title: N meetings in one room
materials.notes: Sort by end time.

materials.title: Candy
materials.notes: Two passes.

materials.title: Check balanced BT
materials.notes: O(n) early-exit return -1 on imbalance.

materials.title: Count nodes in complete BT
materials.notes: O(log²n) with height.

materials.title: Cycle detection in directed (BFS)
materials.notes: Kahn's: if topo length < V, cycle.

materials.title: Shortest path in unit-weight UG
materials.notes: BFS suffices.

materials.title: Number of ways to arrive at dest
Find city with smallest neighbors in threshold
Partition into 2 with min abs diff
0/1 Knapsack
materials.notes: Foundational.

materials.title: Longest palindromic subseq
materials.notes: LCS(s, reverse(s)).

materials.title: LIS using binary search (O(n log n))
Min cost to cut a stick
Max XOR with element from array

tracks.name: Mathematics for AI
tracks.description: Deisenroth/Faisal/Ong MML paired with 3Blue1Brown intuition. 10 chapters spanning linear algebra → optimization → probability.

materials.title: StatQuest — Statistics Fundamentals (playlist)
materials.notes: Josh Starmer's gentle intro: mean/median/variance, distributions, central limit theorem, hypothesis tests. Watch in order; clearly explained.

materials.title: StatQuest — Machine Learning (playlist)
materials.notes: ML from scratch with diagrams: linear/logistic regression, trees, random forest, gradient boost, SVM, NN. Companion to Andrew Ng.

materials.title: Khan Academy — Probability & Statistics
materials.notes: Self-paced. Skip what you know; lock in conditional probability + sampling distributions.

materials.title: Mathematics for Machine Learning Specialization (Coursera)
materials.notes: 3-course Imperial College spec: Linear Algebra → Multivariate Calculus → PCA. Run in parallel with MML book.

materials.title: MML book — Chapter 2: Linear Algebra
materials.notes: Vector spaces, linear independence, basis, rank, linear maps. Work the exercises.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 1: Vectors
materials.notes: Geometric intuition: vector as arrow, as list, as function. Bedrock.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 2: Linear combinations, span, basis
materials.notes: Span = all linear combos. Basis = minimal spanning set.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 3: Linear transformations & matrices
materials.notes: Matrix = where basis vectors land. Multiplication = composition.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 4: Matrix multiplication
materials.notes: Order matters. Composition of transformations.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 6: Determinant
materials.notes: Det = factor by which area/volume scales. Det = 0 iff matrix collapses dimension.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 7: Inverse, column space, null space, rank
materials.notes: rank = dim of column space. null space = solutions to Ax=0. Critical for understanding linear systems.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 9: Dot products & duality
materials.notes: Dot product = projection × length. Connection to dual vectors.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 13: Change of basis
materials.notes: Same transformation, different coordinate systems. P⁻¹AP.

materials.title: 3B1B EOLA — Ch 14: Eigenvectors & eigenvalues
materials.notes: v such that Av = λv. Diagonalization. Core for PCA, attention, stability.

materials.title: Imperial College — Linear Algebra (Coursera)
materials.notes: Optional companion. Module 1-2 cover basics, module 3-5 are applied (PCA, Gram-Schmidt).

materials.title: MML — Chapter 3: Analytic Geometry
materials.notes: Inner products, norms, distances, angles, orthogonal projections, rotations.

materials.title: Orthogonal projection — derivation & code
materials.notes: Project b onto a: ((a·b)/(a·a))·a. Foundation of least squares.

materials.title: Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization
materials.notes: Build orthogonal basis from any basis. Subtract projections.

materials.title: MML — Chapter 4: Matrix Decompositions
materials.notes: Determinant, trace, eigendecomposition, SVD, Cholesky.

materials.title: SVD intuition (3B1B-style)
materials.notes: A = UΣVᵀ. U,V orthogonal. Σ diagonal with singular values. Best low-rank approximation.

materials.title: SVD applications — image compression, recommendations
materials.notes: Truncated SVD keeps top-k singular values. Drastic compression with low loss.

materials.title: Eigendecomposition vs SVD
materials.notes: Eigen needs square + diagonalizable. SVD works for any matrix. PCA = eigen of cov = SVD of centered data.

materials.title: MML — Chapter 5: Vector Calculus
materials.notes: Differentiation, partial derivatives, gradients, Jacobian, Hessian, chain rule, backprop foundation.

materials.title: 3B1B EOC — Ch 4: Chain rule & product rule
materials.notes: Backprop is just the chain rule applied recursively.

materials.title: Gradient & directional derivative
materials.notes: Gradient points in direction of steepest ascent. Magnitude = rate.

materials.title: Jacobian matrix — multivariate derivative
materials.notes: Matrix of partial derivatives. Generalizes derivative to vector-valued functions.

materials.title: Hessian matrix — second-order curvature
materials.notes: Symmetric matrix of 2nd partials. Eigenvalues tell concavity/convexity.

materials.title: Backpropagation from scratch (math)
materials.notes: Compute graph + chain rule. Each node receives gradient from output, distributes to inputs.

materials.title: Matrix calculus cookbook
materials.notes: Reference for derivatives of common matrix expressions. Bookmark.

materials.title: MML — Chapter 6: Probability & Distributions
materials.notes: Probability spaces, conditional, Bayes', expectation, variance, common distributions.

materials.title: Bayes' theorem — intuitive proof (3B1B)
materials.notes: P(H|E) = P(E|H)P(H)/P(E). Backbone of probabilistic ML.

materials.title: Probability distributions overview
materials.notes: Bernoulli, binomial, normal, Poisson, beta, dirichlet, exponential. Know shapes + use cases.

materials.title: Normal distribution & central limit theorem
materials.notes: Why Gaussians appear everywhere. Sum of iids → normal.

materials.title: MLE — maximum likelihood estimation
materials.notes: θ* = argmax P(data|θ). For Gaussian: sample mean & variance. Foundation of training.

materials.title: MAP estimation & Bayesian inference
materials.notes: θ* = argmax P(θ|data) ∝ P(data|θ)P(θ). Prior regularizes.

materials.title: KL divergence & cross-entropy
materials.notes: KL(P||Q) = Σ P(x) log(P(x)/Q(x)). Cross-entropy loss = KL up to constant. Why ML uses it everywhere.

materials.title: Information theory primer (Shannon entropy)
materials.notes: H(X) = -Σ P(x)logP(x). Bits to encode optimally. Mutual information.

materials.title: Conjugate priors
materials.notes: Beta-binomial, Gaussian-Gaussian, Dirichlet-multinomial. Posterior in same family as prior.

materials.title: MML — Chapter 7: Continuous Optimization
materials.notes: Gradient descent, convexity, Lagrange multipliers, KKT conditions, duality.

materials.title: Gradient descent variants (SGD, momentum, Adam)
materials.notes: SGD → momentum → Nesterov → Adagrad → RMSprop → Adam → AdamW. Know what each fixes.

materials.title: Convex optimization intro — Boyd lectures
materials.notes: Stanford EE364A. Bookmark the textbook (free PDF).

materials.title: Why Adam works — visual explanation
materials.notes: Per-parameter learning rates from first/second moments. β1=0.9, β2=0.999 are sensible defaults.

materials.title: Learning rate schedules
materials.notes: Cosine annealing, warmup, one-cycle. LR is the most important hyperparameter.

materials.title: MML — Chapter 9: Linear Regression
materials.notes: Normal equations, MLE view, MAP view, Bayesian linear regression.

materials.title: Linear regression from scratch (NumPy)
materials.notes: Closed form θ = (XᵀX)⁻¹Xᵀy. Then implement with gradient descent.

materials.title: Regularization — ridge & lasso
materials.notes: L2 (ridge) shrinks. L1 (lasso) selects. Both improve generalization.

materials.title: MML — Chapter 10: PCA
materials.notes: Maximize variance OR minimize reconstruction error → same answer. Eigen of cov matrix.

materials.title: PCA step-by-step with code
materials.notes: Center → cov → eigen → project. Or center → SVD → take top-k columns of V.

materials.title: t-SNE & UMAP — nonlinear DR
materials.notes: t-SNE preserves local structure. UMAP faster + better global. Use for viz, not features.

tracks.name: LLM & Machine Learning
tracks.description: Bottom-up: classical ML → neural nets from scratch → transformers → LLM training → RAG → agents. Karpathy + papers + builds.

materials.title: Josh Starmer — StatQuest ML playlist
materials.notes: Bias/variance, all common algorithms, hyperparameter tuning — drawn out with crystal-clear diagrams. Best survey of classical ML.

materials.title: Google — Machine Learning Crash Course
materials.notes: 15-hour fast-paced intro: loss, gradient descent, overfitting, regularization, feature crosses, embeddings, fairness. With Colab labs.

materials.title: Microsoft — Machine Learning for Beginners
materials.notes: 12-week classic ML curriculum with scikit-learn. Useful complement to StatQuest's theory.

materials.title: scikit-learn — User Guide
materials.notes: The canonical Python ML toolbox. Skim sections 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9 — these are the patterns you'll re-use forever.

materials.title: XGBoost — official tutorials
materials.notes: Gradient-boosted trees still beat NN on tabular data. Read 'Introduction to Boosted Trees' first.

materials.title: LightGBM — features doc
materials.notes: Faster than XGBoost via histogram-based binning + leaf-wise growth. Drop-in API.

materials.title: CatBoost — official tutorial
materials.notes: Native categorical handling. Often wins Kaggle without much tuning.

materials.title: Hands-On Machine Learning — Géron (book)
materials.notes: Book + free notebooks. Ch 1-9 = classical ML, Ch 10-19 = deep learning. THE one ML book to own.

materials.title: Andrew Ng — ML Specialization Course 1 (supervised)
materials.notes: Linear/logistic regression, gradient descent, regularization. The canonical first course.

materials.title: Andrew Ng — ML Specialization Course 2 (advanced)
materials.notes: Neural nets in TF, decision trees, XGBoost. Practical ML beyond linear.

materials.title: Andrew Ng — ML Specialization Course 3 (unsupervised + RL)
materials.notes: Clustering, anomaly detection, collaborative filtering, intro RL.

materials.title: 3B1B — Neural networks Ch 1: What is a neural network?
materials.notes: Visual intuition for MLPs. Layers, weights, biases.

materials.title: 3B1B — Neural networks Ch 2: Gradient descent
materials.notes: How learning happens. Cost landscape.

materials.title: 3B1B — Neural networks Ch 3: Backpropagation calculus
materials.notes: The math of backprop. Watch alongside Karpathy's micrograd.

materials.title: 3B1B — Neural networks Ch 4: Backprop algorithm
materials.notes: Algorithmic perspective.

materials.title: Karpathy — micrograd (autograd from scratch)
materials.notes: Build PyTorch's autograd engine in 100 LoC. After this, backprop will never confuse you again.

materials.title: Karpathy — makemore Part 1: bigram model
materials.notes: Character-level name generator. PyTorch basics, embeddings, NLL loss.

materials.title: Karpathy — makemore Part 2: MLP (Bengio 2003)
materials.notes: Implement Bengio's neural language model. Lookup tables, hidden layers.

materials.title: Karpathy — makemore Part 3: activations, gradients, BN
materials.notes: Diagnose dead neurons, saturating tanh, internal covariate shift. Batch norm explained.

materials.title: Karpathy — makemore Part 4: backprop manually
materials.notes: Reimplement backprop for cross-entropy + batch norm by hand. Brutal but illuminating.

materials.title: Karpathy — Let's build GPT from scratch
materials.notes: 2h video. Build transformer + train on tinyshakespeare. THE most important video for understanding LLMs.

materials.title: Karpathy — GPT-2 reproduction (124M)
materials.notes: 4h video. Full GPT-2 reproduction. Distributed training, FlashAttention, mixed precision.

materials.title: Karpathy — Tokenization deep dive
materials.notes: BPE from scratch. Why tokenization is the source of most LLM weirdness.

materials.title: Karpathy — Intro to LLMs (1h talk)
materials.notes: Best high-level intro to how LLMs work. Watch first.

materials.title: nanoGPT — train your own GPT-2
materials.notes: Clone, train on Shakespeare, then OpenWebText. ~$10 of cloud GPU.

materials.title: PyTorch — 60-Minute Blitz
materials.notes: Official quickstart: tensors, autograd, nn.Module, training loop, classifier. Do every cell.

materials.title: PyTorch — Learn the Basics (full tutorial)
materials.notes: Deeper than the blitz: datasets/dataloaders, transforms, save/load, TensorBoard. Set baseline fluency.

materials.title: Fast.ai — Practical Deep Learning for Coders v5
materials.notes: Top-down approach: build CNN classifiers + tabular + NLP before theory. Jeremy Howard. 9 lessons.

materials.title: Andrew Ng — Deep Learning Specialization
materials.notes: 5 courses: NN basics → hyperparameters → ML projects → CNN → sequence models. Slow but bulletproof foundation.

materials.title: StatQuest — Neural Networks playlist
materials.notes: Visual NN deep-dive: backprop, activation, dropout, batch norm. Complementary to 3B1B.

materials.title: Keras / TensorFlow — Sequential Model tutorial
materials.notes: Optional: most ML jobs use PyTorch now, but Keras shows up in legacy/research codebases.

materials.title: "Attention is All You Need" (Vaswani et al.)
materials.notes: The transformer paper. Read 3 times. Self-attention = O(n²) but parallel. Multi-head = multiple subspaces.

materials.title: The Illustrated Transformer (Jay Alammar)
materials.notes: Best visual companion to Attention is All You Need.

materials.title: The Annotated Transformer (Harvard)
materials.notes: Implement the paper line by line with the paper text inline. Gold.

materials.title: GPT-1 paper (Radford 2018)
materials.notes: Decoder-only transformer + unsupervised pretraining + supervised fine-tuning. Started everything.

materials.title: GPT-2 paper (Radford 2019)
materials.notes: Zero-shot task transfer. 1.5B params.

materials.title: GPT-3 paper (Brown et al. 2020)
materials.notes: In-context learning. 175B. Section 3 is the gold (eval suite).

materials.title: BERT paper (Devlin et al.)
materials.notes: Encoder-only, masked LM. Was dominant for NLU before decoder LLMs took over.

materials.title: LLaMA paper (Touvron et al.)
materials.notes: Efficient open LLM. RMSNorm, SwiGLU, RoPE, GQA.

materials.title: LLaMA 2 paper
materials.notes: RLHF details. Ghost attention. 70B SOTA at time.

materials.title: Mistral 7B paper
materials.notes: Sliding window attention, grouped query attention. Punching above weight.

materials.title: Hugging Face — LLM course
materials.notes: Free, hands-on. Tokenizers → Transformers → Datasets → Fine-tuning.

materials.title: Lilian Weng — The Transformer Family (v2)
materials.notes: Encyclopedia of transformer variants. Reference.

materials.title: InstructGPT paper (Ouyang et al.)
materials.notes: SFT → RM training → PPO. The recipe that made ChatGPT work.

materials.title: RLHF — Hugging Face deep dive
materials.notes: Practical implementation walk-through. Use TRL library.

materials.title: DPO paper (Rafailov et al.)
materials.notes: Direct Preference Optimization. Skips reward model, optimizes preferences directly. Simpler than PPO.

materials.title: Constitutional AI (Anthropic)
materials.notes: Self-supervised alignment via principles. Used by Claude.

materials.title: LoRA paper
materials.notes: Low-rank adapters: train tiny matrices that perturb frozen weights. The standard for fine-tuning.

materials.title: QLoRA paper
materials.notes: 4-bit quantization + LoRA. Fine-tune 65B on a single GPU.

materials.title: PEFT library — hands-on
materials.notes: Apply LoRA/QLoRA/IA³ to any HF model in 5 lines.

materials.title: RAG — Lewis et al.
materials.notes: Original retrieval-augmented generation paper. Retriever + generator joint training.

materials.title: ReAct paper (Yao et al.)
materials.notes: Reason + Act = interleaved thought and action. Foundation of modern agents.

materials.title: Chain-of-Thought paper
materials.notes: "Let's think step by step." Emergent at scale (>60B).

materials.title: Tree of Thoughts
materials.notes: Search over CoT branches. BFS/DFS with self-eval.

materials.title: Toolformer
materials.notes: Self-supervised tool learning. Pre-cursor to function calling.

materials.title: LangChain docs (intro)
materials.notes: Practical orchestration. Note: API churns; build your own loops once you grok it.

materials.title: LlamaIndex docs
materials.notes: Better than LangChain for RAG-focused pipelines. Indexes + query engines.

materials.title: Build a RAG system from scratch
materials.notes: Capstone: ingest your own corpus → chunk → embed → retrieve → generate → eval. End-to-end ownership.

materials.title: vLLM — production LLM serving
materials.notes: PagedAttention. Continuous batching. The standard for OSS LLM serving.

materials.title: Lilian Weng — LLM Powered Autonomous Agents
materials.notes: Comprehensive overview of agent architectures. Read before building.

materials.title: Stanford CS231n — Deep Learning for Computer Vision
materials.notes: The CV course. Lecture videos + assignments. CNNs → object detection → segmentation → generative.

materials.title: Stanford CS224n — NLP with Deep Learning
materials.notes: The NLP course. Word vectors → RNN → seq2seq → attention → transformers. Watch alongside Karpathy.

materials.title: Hugging Face — NLP Course
materials.notes: Hands-on NLP with Transformers + Datasets + Tokenizers libraries. Free, practical, fast.

materials.title: Hugging Face — Deep RL Course
materials.notes: Q-learning → DQN → policy gradients → PPO → multi-agent. Hands-on with stable-baselines3 and ML-Agents.

materials.title: Berkeley CS285 — Deep Reinforcement Learning
materials.notes: The rigorous DRL course. Sergey Levine. Pair with Sutton & Barto. Optional unless you want RL depth.

materials.title: Spinning Up in Deep RL — OpenAI
materials.notes: Best practical entry point to DRL: VPG → TRPO → PPO → DDPG → SAC. Clean code + intuitive write-ups.

materials.title: GANs — Goodfellow et al. (2014)
materials.notes: The original adversarial generator paper. Foundation for everything pre-diffusion.

materials.title: DDPM — Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (Ho et al.)
materials.notes: The diffusion model paper that made Stable Diffusion possible. Read with Lilian Weng's blog open.

materials.title: Lilian Weng — What are Diffusion Models?
materials.notes: The clearest written explanation of DDPM. Math + intuition.

materials.title: Stable Diffusion — Latent Diffusion paper
materials.notes: Run diffusion in latent space (VAE-compressed) → 10× speedup → SD becomes feasible on consumer GPUs.

materials.title: Microsoft — Generative AI for Beginners
materials.notes: 21-lesson tour: prompting, RAG, embeddings, fine-tuning, agents. Light but broad.

materials.title: Lilian Weng — A Visual Guide to LLM Agents
materials.notes: (Reprise — also in Phase 4.) Reference when building agentic systems.

materials.title: A Visual Guide to Mixture of Experts (MoE)
materials.notes: MoE: route tokens to a subset of experts. Used by Mixtral, GPT-4, DeepSeek-V3. Visual + clear.

materials.title: Understanding Reasoning LLMs — Sebastian Raschka
materials.notes: What changed in o1, R1, and reasoning models. Test-time compute, CoT distillation, RL on reasoning traces.

materials.title: A Visual Guide to Reasoning LLMs
materials.notes: Visual companion to Raschka's piece. Shows the inference-time scaling curves.

materials.title: Understanding Multimodal LLMs — Sebastian Raschka
materials.notes: How vision encoders attach to LLMs (CLIP, Flamingo, LLaVA, GPT-4V). Cross-attention vs early fusion.

materials.title: The Illustrated GPT-2 — Jay Alammar
materials.notes: Visual GPT-2 walkthrough. Read after the original Illustrated Transformer.

materials.title: Understanding Large Language Models — Raschka
materials.notes: Concise survey of LLM architectures + training. Good single-page reference.

materials.title: Hugging Face — LLM Course (modules 6-9 advanced)
materials.notes: (Reprise — see Phase 3.) Continue with advanced modules: long-context, multilingual, code, alignment.

materials.title: RAG Techniques — NirDiamant (GitHub)
materials.notes: Best catalog of advanced RAG patterns with code. Bookmark + work through 5-10 of them after you own a basic RAG.

materials.title: Precise Zero-Shot Dense Retrieval — HyDE
materials.notes: Generate a hypothetical answer with LLM → embed it → retrieve. Often beats direct query embedding.

materials.title: ColBERT — late-interaction retrieval
materials.notes: Per-token embeddings + max-sim. Much better recall than single-vector for long passages.

materials.title: Self-RAG — Asai et al.
materials.notes: LLM emits special tokens to decide when/what to retrieve and whether to critique its own outputs.

materials.title: Re-ranking with cross-encoders
materials.notes: Two-stage: dense recall → cross-encoder rerank top-50. Huge accuracy boost for retrieval.

materials.title: Anthropic — Contextual Retrieval
materials.notes: Prepend chunk-specific context via LLM before embedding. ~49% reduction in failed retrievals.

materials.title: Awesome LLM Apps — RAG examples
materials.notes: Catalog of end-to-end RAG apps with code. Inspiration + working starter kits.

materials.title: Evaluating RAG with RAGAS
materials.notes: Faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision/recall — without ground truth labels. Critical for iteration.

materials.title: Hugging Face — AI Agents Course
materials.notes: Free, hands-on. Build agents from scratch → smolagents → LangGraph. Best comprehensive agents course right now.

materials.title: Chip Huyen — Agents
materials.notes: The clearest definition of an agent in 2025. Tool use → planning → reflection. Read twice.

materials.title: Building AI Browser Agents — DeepLearning.AI
materials.notes: Multi-step browser automation with computer use. Real-world action-taking agents.

materials.title: Anthropic — Model Context Protocol guide
materials.notes: Open standard for connecting LLMs to tools/data. Becoming the agent integration layer.

materials.title: Hugging Face — MCP Course
materials.notes: Build MCP servers + clients. Connect Claude/Cursor/your-agent to your own tools.

materials.title: Awesome MCP Servers
materials.notes: Catalog of community MCP servers (filesystem, GitHub, Slack, Postgres, …). Steal patterns for your own.

materials.title: AI Agents in Action (Manning)
materials.notes: Book — end-to-end agent systems: memory, planning, tools, multi-agent. Optional long-form.

materials.title: Build a Multi-Agent System from Scratch (Manning)
materials.notes: Companion book — orchestration, coordination, shared state. Optional.

materials.title: LLM eval frameworks — overview
materials.notes: Eugene Yan's catalog of patterns. Pragmatic, battle-tested.

materials.title: Prompt engineering guide
materials.notes: Catalog of techniques: few-shot, CoT, ReAct, self-consistency, ToT.

materials.title: Anthropic — Building effective agents
materials.notes: Workflows vs agents. When to use which. Patterns: chain, route, parallelize, orchestrator-workers.

materials.title: Anthropic — Tool use docs
materials.notes: JSON schema-defined tools. Multi-turn tool use. Parallel tool execution.

materials.title: OpenAI cookbook — function calling
materials.notes: Same idea, OpenAI API. Convergent design across providers.

materials.title: Andrew Ng — Machine Learning in Production (DeepLearning.AI)
materials.notes: 4-course MLOps spec: project scoping → data → modeling → deployment. THE practical MLOps course.

materials.title: Full Stack Deep Learning
materials.notes: Pragmatic course on shipping ML systems. Data labeling, experiment tracking, deployment, monitoring.

materials.title: Stanford CS329S — ML Systems Design
materials.notes: Chip Huyen's course. ML system design at scale. Pair with her 'Designing ML Systems' book.

materials.title: MLflow — official tutorials
materials.notes: Experiment tracking + model registry + serving. Lightweight, open, ubiquitous.

materials.title: Streamlit — get started
materials.notes: Fastest way to put a UI on a model demo. Useful for prototypes + internal tools.

materials.title: vLLM — production LLM serving
materials.notes: (Reprise.) PagedAttention + continuous batching. The OSS serving standard.

materials.title: Designing ML Systems — Chip Huyen (book)
materials.notes: System design for ML in industry. Data, training, serving, monitoring, organization.

materials.title: AI Engineering — Chip Huyen (book, 2024)
materials.notes: The LLM-era successor. Foundation models, evaluation, RAG, fine-tuning, agents, production patterns. Current.

materials.title: Build a Large Language Model From Scratch — Sebastian Raschka
materials.notes: The book version of Karpathy's videos with extra rigor: data → tokenizer → architecture → pretraining → fine-tuning → RLHF. Capstone build.

materials.title: Deep Learning — Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville
materials.notes: The reference. Math-heavy. Use as a lookup, not linear read. Part II ch 6-9 = MLP/CNN/RNN. Part III = research.

materials.title: Deep Learning with Python — Chollet (2nd ed)
materials.notes: Keras-focused. Useful for the conceptual chapters; skip if you're staying in PyTorch.

materials.title: Natural Language Processing with Transformers (HF book)
materials.notes: HF team's book. Hands-on: classification, NER, QA, summarization, generation with HF Transformers.

materials.title: Why Machines Learn — Anil Ananthaswamy
materials.notes: Optional: lay-mathematical narrative of how ML evolved. Good for context, not skills.

materials.title: LLMs in Production — Manning
materials.notes: Production-focused: latency, observability, prompt versioning, cost. Complements Chip Huyen's AI Engineering.

materials.title: Prompt Engineering for LLMs — Berryman & Ziegler (O'Reilly)
materials.notes: Beyond catalog: structured techniques for production prompts. Useful for agent system design.

materials.title: Reference list — Must-Read AI Papers (curated)
materials.notes: Source list for many materials here. Bookmark for new additions.

tracks.name: System Design
tracks.description: LLD patterns → distributed building blocks → HLD case studies → GenAI infrastructure. From parking-lot to vLLM serving.

materials.title: Hello Interview — System Design in a Hurry (playlist)
materials.notes: Start here. Modern, interview-focused, sharp. Watch entire 'System Design in a Hurry' playlist.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Core Concepts: Scaling, Sharding, CAP
materials.notes: Foundational vocabulary every SD interview assumes you know cold.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Key Technologies (DBs, Caches, Queues)
materials.notes: The toolbox you compose answers from. Postgres vs Dynamo vs Cassandra; Redis vs Memcached; Kafka vs SQS.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Common Patterns (Read/Write Heavy, Geo)
materials.notes: Pattern recognition shortcuts. Most SD problems map to 5-6 archetypes.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Delivery Framework
materials.notes: Requirements → API → data model → high level → deep dives. The 4-step interview structure. Memorize this.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design TicketMaster
materials.notes: High-contention seat reservation. Optimistic vs pessimistic locking. Redis distributed locks.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design Uber
materials.notes: Geo-spatial indexing (S2/H3/Quadtree). Driver location streams. Matching algorithm.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design Top K (Leaderboard)
materials.notes: Count-min sketch + heap. Approximate algorithms for scale.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design Yelp / Proximity Service
materials.notes: Geohash. Quadtree vs S2 vs H3 tradeoffs.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design WhatsApp
materials.notes: Persistent WebSocket. Message delivery semantics. E2E encryption.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design FB Live Comments
materials.notes: SSE vs WebSocket vs long-poll. Fanout strategies.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design Dropbox
materials.notes: Block storage. Chunking + delta sync. Conflict resolution.

materials.title: Hello Interview — Design Web Crawler
materials.notes: URL frontier. Politeness + duplicate URL detection. Bloom filters.

materials.title: ByteByteGo — System Design 101 (playlist)
materials.notes: Alex Xu's animated explanations. Watch the System Design 101 playlist start-to-finish.

materials.title: ByteByteGo — Top 5 Most Used Architecture Patterns
materials.notes: Layered, Event-Driven, Microservices, Microkernel, Space-Based.

materials.title: ByteByteGo — How does Redis really work?
ByteByteGo — Database Indexing Explained
materials.notes: B-tree vs hash index. Composite indexes. When indexes hurt.

materials.title: Gaurav Sen — System Design Playlist (35+ videos)
materials.notes: Classic case studies. Watch in order. The 'distributed systems' subset is excellent.

materials.title: Gaurav Sen — Consistent Hashing
materials.notes: The canonical 15min explanation. Watch twice.

materials.title: Gaurav Sen — Distributed Counter
materials.notes: CRDTs, eventual consistency.

materials.title: Jordan Has No Life — System Design Interview (playlist)
materials.notes: The most detailed free SD playlist on YouTube. Methodical, brutal honesty about tradeoffs.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Distributed Systems (full course, free)
materials.notes: THE gold standard distributed systems course. 20 lectures, Robert Morris (MIT). Theory deep enough that everything else feels shallow. Spread across 4 weeks; this is your moat.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Lecture 1: Introduction (MapReduce)
materials.notes: Foundations: why distributed, MapReduce paper walkthrough.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Lecture 3: GFS
materials.notes: Google File System. Master + chunkservers. Single-master tradeoffs.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Lecture 6: Raft basics
materials.notes: Raft consensus paper walkthrough. Leader election. Required reading.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Lecture 8: ZooKeeper
materials.notes: Coordination service. Strong consistency primitives others build on.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Lecture 12: Distributed transactions
materials.notes: 2PC, OCC, snapshot isolation.

materials.title: MIT 6.824 — Lecture 13: Spanner
materials.notes: Google's globally distributed strongly consistent DB. TrueTime.

materials.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications (book)
materials.notes: Martin Kleppmann. THE textbook. Schedule 1 chapter per week alongside videos. Non-negotiable.

materials.title: SOLID principles
materials.notes: SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP. Every code review should reference these.

materials.title: Refactoring Guru — Design patterns (all 23)
materials.notes: Read all. Memorize: Strategy, Observer, Factory, Singleton, Decorator, Adapter, Command. Use cards for the rest.

materials.title: LLD — Parking Lot
materials.notes: Vehicles, slots, tickets, payment. Class diagram + Python impl. Classic warmup.

materials.title: LLD — Splitwise
materials.notes: Users, groups, expenses, balances. Observer pattern for notifications.

materials.title: LLD — Elevator
materials.notes: State machine. SCAN/LOOK scheduling. Multiple elevator coordination.

materials.title: LLD — Vending Machine (State pattern)
materials.notes: States: idle, has-money, dispensing. Transitions. Strategy pattern alternative.

materials.title: LLD — Cab booking (Uber)
materials.notes: Strategy for fare. Observer for notifications. State for ride lifecycle.

materials.title: System Design Primer (Donne Martin)
materials.notes: THE single best free system design resource. Read sections in order. ~8h total. Internalize.

materials.title: Load balancers — L4 vs L7
materials.notes: Round robin, least conn, IP hash, consistent hash. L4 (TCP) vs L7 (HTTP) tradeoffs.

materials.title: CAP theorem & PACELC
materials.notes: In partition: Consistency or Availability. PACELC adds: else, Latency or Consistency. Know examples (DynamoDB AP, Spanner CP).

materials.title: SQL vs NoSQL — when to use which
materials.notes: Relational for joins + ACID. KV/Document/Column for scale + flexible schema. Hybrid is the norm.

materials.title: Database sharding strategies
materials.notes: Range, hash, geo. Hotspots, resharding, distributed transactions are the pain.

materials.title: Database replication — leader-follower, multi-leader
materials.notes: Sync vs async replication. Lag. Failover. Quorum reads.

materials.title: Consistency models
materials.notes: Strong, sequential, causal, eventual. Jepsen's diagram is gospel.

materials.title: Caching patterns
materials.notes: Cache-aside, read-through, write-through, write-back, refresh-ahead. Invalidation. Stampede.

materials.title: Redis use cases & data structures
materials.notes: Strings, lists, sets, sorted sets, hashes, streams. Pub/sub. Use cases: cache, rate limit, leaderboard, queue.

materials.title: Message queues — Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs SQS
materials.notes: Kafka: durable log, replay, high throughput. RabbitMQ: routing, complex topology. SQS: managed simple.

materials.title: Kafka deep dive — partitions, consumer groups
materials.notes: Topics → partitions → offsets. Consumer groups for parallel + at-least-once. Exactly-once semantics.

materials.title: Consistent hashing
materials.notes: Ring, virtual nodes. Minimal reshuffling on node add/remove. Used in DynamoDB, Cassandra, CDNs.

materials.title: Rate limiting algorithms
materials.notes: Token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, sliding window log, sliding window counter.

materials.title: REST vs gRPC vs GraphQL
materials.notes: REST: simple, ubiquitous. gRPC: binary, streaming, internal. GraphQL: client-shaped queries, federation.

materials.title: HLD — TinyURL / URL shortener
materials.notes: Hash vs counter for ID. Base62 encoding. Read-heavy: cache. Custom URLs.

materials.title: HLD — Pastebin
materials.notes: Object storage for content. Metadata in DB. Expiration job.

materials.title: HLD — Twitter feed
materials.notes: Fanout-on-write vs fanout-on-read. Hybrid for celebrities. Timeline cache.

materials.title: HLD — Instagram
materials.notes: Photo storage (CDN), metadata, feed, search. Encoding pipeline.

materials.title: HLD — Netflix / video streaming
materials.notes: Transcoding pipeline. ABR (adaptive bitrate). CDN edge caches. Recommendation.

materials.title: HLD — WhatsApp / chat
materials.notes: WebSocket persistent connection. Message broker. Delivery + read receipts. E2E encryption.

materials.title: HLD — Uber / ride dispatch
materials.notes: Geo sharding (S2/H3). Driver location streams. Matching algorithm. Surge pricing.

materials.title: HLD — Dropbox / cloud storage
materials.notes: Block-level dedup. Delta sync. Conflict resolution.

materials.title: HLD — Distributed cache (memcached/Redis cluster)
materials.notes: Consistent hashing. Replication for fault tolerance. Cache stampede prevention.

materials.title: HLD — Rate limiter (distributed)
materials.notes: Redis Lua scripts for atomicity. Token bucket per user. Sliding window for accuracy.

materials.title: HLD — Notification system
materials.notes: Provider abstraction (push, email, SMS). Templates. Retry + dedup. User preferences.

materials.title: HLD — Search autocomplete
materials.notes: Trie + top-k per prefix. Update pipeline. Distribute by prefix sharding.

materials.title: HLD — Web crawler
materials.notes: URL frontier (BFS with politeness). Dedup via bloom filters. Content extraction.

materials.title: HLD — YouTube
materials.notes: Upload pipeline → transcoding → CDN. Recommendation. Live streaming.

materials.title: HLD — Distributed unique ID (Snowflake)
materials.notes: 64-bit: timestamp + machine ID + sequence. Sortable, unique, no central coordinator.

materials.title: Designing ML systems — book overview
materials.notes: Chip Huyen. Best book on production ML systems. Skim TOC, deep-read chapters 4-7.

materials.title: Eugene Yan — System design for ML platforms
vLLM internals — PagedAttention paper
materials.notes: KV cache as virtual memory pages. 2-4x throughput vs naive. Foundation of modern serving.

materials.title: Continuous batching — Anyscale post
materials.notes: Iteration-level scheduling. Why static batching is suboptimal.

materials.title: Speculative decoding
materials.notes: Draft model + verify with big model. 2-3x decode speedup.

materials.title: Building RAG at scale (Pinecone)
materials.notes: Embedding model choice. Chunk sizing. Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + vector). Reranking.

materials.title: LLM observability — Eugene Yan
materials.notes: LLM-as-judge. Pitfalls (position bias, verbosity bias). Calibration.

materials.title: ByteByteGo newsletter (subscribe)
materials.notes: Weekly distributed systems + GenAI infra deep dives.
